title: Background Tasks — Parallel Execution
description: The agent runs a long-running process (like tests or builds) in the background while continuing other work. A notification arrives when the background task completes, allowing the agent to react to results without blocking.

steps.content: Run the full test suite and while it's running, add a new endpoint GET /books/{id} to BookController.
steps.annotation: The user wants two things done in parallel: run tests (slow) and add code (fast). Background tasks enable this.

steps.content: I'll start the test suite in the background and work on the new endpoint while it runs.
steps.annotation: The agent plans to use a background task for the slow operation (tests) so it can continue working.
The agent launches 'mvn test' as a background task. Unlike a normal bash call, this returns immediately — the agent doesn't wait for the tests to finish.

steps.content:
Background task started [id=bg-9x2k]: 'mvn test -pl bookstore-api'
Status: RUNNING. You will be notified when it completes.
steps.annotation: The system confirms the background task is running. The agent receives a task ID it can reference later. Execution continues immediately.
While tests run in the background, the agent reads the controller to add the new endpoint. No waiting required.
Controller read. The agent will add the GET /books/{id} endpoint.
Adding the GET /books/{id} endpoint. This work happens concurrently with the background test run — no time wasted waiting.
Endpoint added. The agent completed its foreground work while tests run in the background.

steps.content:
Background task completed [id=bg-9x2k]: 'mvn test -pl bookstore-api'
Status: SUCCESS (exit code 0)
Duration: 34s
Output (last 5 lines):
  Tests run: 12, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0
  BUILD SUCCESS
  Total time: 34.217 s
steps.annotation: The background task completion notification arrives. All 12 tests passed. The agent can now incorporate this result into its response.

steps.content:
Done! Two things completed in parallel:

1. **New endpoint added** — `GET /books/{id}` returns a book by ID with proper 404 handling
2. **Test suite passed** — All 12 tests passed (34s)

The new endpoint returns `ResponseEntity<Book>` so it sends a 404 status when the book isn't found, rather than returning null.
steps.annotation: Background tasks let the agent overlap slow operations (builds, tests, deployments) with fast operations (editing, reading). This reduces total wall-clock time for complex workflows.
